headline: Amazons delivery, logistics get an AI boost
summary: SUNNYVALE, Calif. (Reuters) -Amazon wants customers to know that artificial intelligence is not just for writing college essays.  In a series of announcements Wednesday, Amazon demonstrated how stockroom robots, delivery people and its sprawling warehouses will all benefit from a hefty dose of artificial intelligence, speeding packages to customer doorsteps.  Using so-called agentic AI, these robots will be able to unload trailers and then retrieve parts for repairs, according to Amazon.

headline: Amazon cuts more jobs, this time in books division
summary: SAN FRANCISCO (Reuters) -Amazon has cut jobs in its books division, including at its Goodreads review site and Kindle units, the company said on Thursday.  The Seattle-based retailer said that fewer than 100 workers were impacted and the move was meant to improve efficiency and streamline operations.  As part of our ongoing work to make our teams and programs operate more efficiently, and to better align with our business roadmap, we’ve made the difficult decision to eliminate a small number of roles within the Books organization, an Amazon spokesperson said in a statement.

headline: Walmart and Amazon Are Exploring Issuing Their Own Stablecoins
summary: Walmart  Amazon.com and other multinational giants have recently explored whether to issue their own stablecoins in the U.S., according to people familiar with the matter.
